summary: Research on the cultivation and commercialization of *Chlorella vulgaris*, *Spirulina platensis*, and *Haematococcus pluvialis* was synthesized, revealing a cluster focused on optimizing production methods for high-value compounds. Key findings centered on the historical progression from fundamental science to industrial applications, particularly in nutraceuticals, cosmetics, and biofuel sectors. The studies collectively emphasized strategies to enhance biomass yield and extract bioactive molecules, though geographic scope remained broad and unspecified. This thematic cluster underscores the ongoing effort to standardize cultivation techniques to unlock the full commercial potential of these microalgae species.
